description_long: This bollard is a cylindrical, robust post with a dark, weathered wooden appearance and a rounded cap. The surface shows signs of age, with visible grain, discoloration, and some small holes or pitting on the top. One side features a prominent shield-shaped plaque bordered in gold, with partially legible text reading 'NUNX VONC' or similar, suggesting a decorative or commemorative function. The bollard stands on a flat, square base that appears to be made of concrete or stone, providing stability. The overall design is utilitarian but with a touch of ornamentation, likely intended for use in historic or themed urban environments. There are no visible chains, connectors, or moving parts, and the object appears solid and heavy.
description: A cylindrical, dark wooden bollard with a rounded top and a gold-bordered shield plaque on one side, mounted on a flat square base. The surface is weathered and aged, giving it a historic or decorative look.
description_view_0: Front view shows the plain cylindrical body and rounded cap, with no visible plaque or ornamentation.
description_view_1: This view highlights the gold-bordered shield plaque with text, making it the most decorative side.
description_view_2: The shield plaque is visible from an angle, showing more of the text and the curvature of the bollard.
description_view_3: Rear view displays the plain, weathered wooden surface without any plaques or markings.
